message:
feat(core,docs): implement hydrology tile layer infrastructure for water simulation foundation

This commit implements the foundational hydrology tile system outlined in the PLAN.md blueprint,
enabling per-tile water flow properties as the first phase of the dynamic rainstorm feature.

## Hydrology Tile Schema (core/lib.rs +129 lines)

**TileSpec Extensions (5 new optional fields):**

- `permeability: Option<f32>` (0.0-1.0) - How quickly water infiltrates into ground
  - Sand: 0.8 (very permeable), Rock: 0.1 (nearly impermeable), Grass: 0.6 (moderate)
  - Controls absorption rate during rainfall events

- `runoff_bias: Option<f32>` (-1.0 to 1.0) - Surface water retention vs. drainage tendency
  - DeepWater: +0.9 (accumulates water), Sand: -0.2 (sheds water), Bloom: -0.1 (slight shedding)
  - Positive values indicate basins/depressions, negative indicates drainage slopes

- `basin_rank: Option<f32>` (0.0-1.0) - Relative importance as water collector
  - DeepWater: 1.0 (primary basin), ShallowWater: 0.8 (secondary), Rock: 0.2 (minor)
  - Used by flow solver to prioritize basin outlets during flooding

- `channel_priority: Option<f32>` (0.0-1.0) - Likelihood to become part of drainage network
  - Rock: 0.7 (natural channels), ShallowWater: 0.6 (waterways), DeepWater: 0.2 (endpoints)
  - Guides D8 flow accumulation algorithm toward geologically coherent rivers

- `swim_cost: Option<f32>` (0.0+) - Movement penalty when tile is flooded
  - DeepWater: 0.0 (native swimming), Sand: 2.0 (heavy penalty), Rock: 2.5 (nearly impassable)
  - Integrated with agent pathfinding during flood events

All fields use `#[serde(default)]` for backward compatibility with existing tilesets.

**HydrologyTile & HydrologyTileLayer Structs:**

Created dedicated containers for hydrology metadata parallel to TerrainLayer:

- `HydrologyTile` - Per-cell struct storing the 5 hydrology properties
- `HydrologyTileLayer` - 2D grid wrapper with width/height/tiles
- Both fully serializable (Serialize + Deserialize) for artifact persistence
- Accessor methods: `width()`, `height()`, `tiles()` for read-only access

**MapArtifact Integration:**

- Added `hydrology_tiles: Option<HydrologyTileLayer>` field to MapArtifact
- Generator now builds hydrology layer alongside terrain/fertility/temperature
- New accessor: `hydrology_tiles()` returns `Option<&HydrologyTileLayer>`
- Public exports extended: `HydrologyTile`, `HydrologyTileLayer` now part of API

**CompiledTile Expansion:**

Added 5 hydrology fields to internal CompiledTile struct used during WFC generation:
- All fields populated with sensible terrain-kind-specific defaults when unspecified
- Clamping logic: permeability/basin_rank/channel_priority to [0.0, 1.0]
- runoff_bias clamped to [-1.0, 1.0], swim_cost clamped to [0.0, ∞)

**Default Value Functions (5 new helpers):**

Implemented terrain-kind-specific defaults when tileset omits hydrology fields:

1. `default_permeability_for_kind(kind)`:
   - Water: very low (0.05-0.15) - already saturated
   - Sand: high (0.8) - drains quickly
   - Grass/Bloom: moderate (0.5-0.6) - natural soils
   - Rock: very low (0.1) - impermeable

2. `default_runoff_bias_for_kind(kind)`:
   - DeepWater: strongly positive (+0.9) - natural accumulator
   - ShallowWater: positive (+0.6) - minor basin
   - Grass/Sand: slightly negative (-0.1 to -0.2) - drains to neighbors
   - Rock: positive (+0.5) - channels water but doesn't absorb

3. `default_basin_rank_for_kind(kind)`:
   - DeepWater: 1.0 - primary basin
   - ShallowWater/Bloom: 0.55-0.8 - secondary collectors
   - Sand/Grass: 0.35-0.4 - tertiary
   - Rock: 0.2 - minimal collection

4. `default_channel_priority_for_kind(kind)`:
   - Rock: 0.7 - natural channels (gorges/ravines)
   - ShallowWater: 0.6 - existing waterways
   - Grass: 0.5 - neutral
   - DeepWater: 0.2 - endpoints not channels

5. `default_swim_cost_for_kind(kind)`:
   - DeepWater: 0.0 - no swimming penalty (native medium)
   - ShallowWater: 0.3 - minor wading penalty
   - Grass/Bloom: 1.2-1.5 - moderate flooded vegetation penalty
   - Sand/Rock: 2.0-2.5 - heavy penalty when waterlogged

**Generation Pipeline Updates:**

Modified `RuleBasedMapGenerator::generate()` to build hydrology data:
- Allocates `hydrology_tiles` vector with same capacity as terrain
- Populates HydrologyTile for each WFC-placed tile using compiled properties
- Wraps result in HydrologyTileLayer with matching dimensions
- Attaches to MapArtifact alongside existing fertility/temperature fields

**Test Coverage:**

Extended WFC unit test to verify hydrology layer presence:
- Asserts `artifact.hydrology_tiles()` returns Some
- Validates dimensions match terrain (8×8)
- Confirms non-zero permeability values exist (basic sanity check)

## Terminal Test Formatting (terminal/mod.rs +6 lines)

Reformatted two multi-line assert! statements for consistency:
- `auto_pause_on_max_age()` assert split across 4 lines
- `auto_pause_on_population_threshold()` assert split across 4 lines
- Improves readability and matches Rust formatting conventions

## Documentation (PLAN.md +1 line)

Marked "Hydrology & Dynamic Water Systems" section as:
`[Currently In Progress - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23]`

Indicates active development of the blueprint's first implementation phase.

## Import Reordering (core/lib.rs -1 Rng import)

- Removed unused `Rng` trait import from `rand08` (only `SeedableRng` needed)
- Reordered imports alphabetically within groups for consistency

This implementation provides the static data layer needed for the upcoming dynamic water
simulation. Future commits will add the flow solver (D8 algorithm, basin detection), the
rainstorm event system (weather controller, water_depth field), and agent interaction
mechanics (swimming trait, pathfinding penalties). The hydrology tiles remain constant
after map generation, serving as terrain constants that influence dynamic water behavior.

Example tileset usage:
```toml
[[tiles]]
id = "wetland"
terrain_kind = "Grass"
permeability = 0.9        # High infiltration
runoff_bias = 0.4         # Slight water retention
basin_rank = 0.7          # Acts as minor basin
channel_priority = 0.3    # Unlikely to channel
swim_cost = 0.8           # Easy to wade when flooded
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+### Hydrology & Dynamic Water Systems (Rainstorms + Floodable Basins) — Proposed Blueprint [Currently In Progress - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23]
 - **Motivation**: Elevate sandbox maps beyond static biomes by layering deterministic hydrology that can react to scripted rainstorms. Agents must negotiate seasonal floods, learn to detour, or evolve swimming. The system needs to dovetail cleanly with WFC generation so basins, rivers, and floodplains emerge coherently and remain reproducible.
